title: High CPU usage on API service
summary: CPU usage consistently above 90% on application servers during peak hours.
description: Logs show repeated expensive DB queries without indexing. Likely cause is inefficient query execution leading to CPU spikes. Evidence includes slow query logs and high query latency. Suggested actions: add indexes, optimize queries, and enable query caching.
notes: Useful for backend API optimization and database tuning scenarios.

title: Memory leak in worker process
summary: Memory usage gradually increases over time until process restart.
description: Heap usage grows continuously with no release. Likely cause is unclosed objects or circular references in background jobs. Evidence includes memory profiling and GC logs. Suggested actions: fix object lifecycle, use memory profiling tools, and enforce periodic restarts.
notes: Common in Celery workers or long-running services.

title: Disk I/O saturation on database node
summary: Disk I/O utilization at 100% with increased query latency.
description: Metrics show high read/write wait times. Likely cause is excessive disk access due to missing caching or heavy writes. Evidence includes iostat logs and DB slow queries. Suggested actions: introduce caching layer, optimize writes, and consider SSD upgrades.

title: Network latency spike between services
description: Tracing shows delays in service-to-service calls. Likely cause is network congestion or DNS resolution delays. Evidence includes tracing spans and network metrics. Suggested actions: check network routes, enable connection pooling, and optimize DNS caching.

title: Thread contention causing slow response
summary: High response time with low CPU utilization.
description: Thread dumps show multiple threads waiting on locks. Likely cause is contention on shared resources. Evidence includes thread state analysis. Suggested actions: reduce lock scope, use async processing, or redesign concurrency model.

title: Frequent garbage collection pauses
summary: Application experiencing latency spikes due to GC pauses.
description: GC logs show frequent full GC cycles. Likely cause is high object allocation rate. Evidence includes GC logs and memory allocation metrics. Suggested actions: tune GC settings, reduce object creation, and increase heap size.
notes: Common in JVM or Python high-allocation workloads.

title: Slow file processing pipeline
summary: Batch jobs taking longer than expected to process files.
description: Logs show blocking file reads and writes. Likely cause is inefficient file handling or disk bottleneck. Evidence includes processing time logs. Suggested actions: use streaming, parallelize processing, and optimize file access patterns.

title: API timeout due to upstream dependency
description: Logs show delays in third-party API responses. Likely cause is slow upstream service. Evidence includes timeout logs and external API latency metrics. Suggested actions: add retries, circuit breakers, and caching.

title: High CPU usage due to infinite loop bug
summary: CPU spikes to 100% immediately after deployment.
description: Logs indicate repeated execution of a loop without exit condition. Likely cause is a code bug. Evidence includes stack traces and logs. Suggested actions: fix loop logic, add safeguards, and implement monitoring alerts.
notes: Classic bug scenario for debugging exercises.

title: Unclassified performance degradation
summary: System performance degraded without clear resource saturation.
description: Metrics show moderate usage across CPU, memory, and I/O. Likely cause is misconfiguration or cascading failures. Evidence includes mixed signals from logs and metrics. Suggested actions: perform holistic system analysis, check recent deployments, and enable detailed tracing.
notes: Useful for ambiguous or complex incidents.
